priority_basis: R-015 bound capability packets to replay windows by navigation identity and wall-clock freshness, but wall-clock timestamps can move because of system clock skew or user adjustment.
action: Implement a fail-closed monotonic clock-domain gate for Event Timing capability and replay evidence.

progress.finding: Same-navigation freshness and chronology must be evaluated with performance.now() and performance.timeOrigin rather than Date.now(); a valid navigation token alone does not prove a shared clock domain.

handoff: Vercel Studio should collect the clock packet in the same navigation as the R-014 capability packet and replay window, require clock_binding_valid and R-015 integration_valid before interpretation, and retain only the privacy-bounded decision output.
limits: No physical iPhone Safari packet was evaluated.
No deployed Vercel integration currently invokes the gate.
The production monotonic freshness budget remains proposed rather than empirically calibrated.
Clock binding does not establish event delivery, replay completeness, responsiveness, visual divergence, or causality.
next_action: Compose R-014 capability validity, R-015 navigation integration, and R-016 monotonic clock binding into one deployed replay-acceptance gate, then test reload, backgrounding, and wall-clock-adjustment cases on physical iPhone Safari.
